title: cyathostemon divaricatus
intro: Cyathostemon divaricatus, also known as the 'bushy paperbark', is a small to medium-sized shrub native to Australia, characterized by its papery bark and small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cyathostemon divaricatus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusion of the leaves and branches, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical treatment.
